heading: Cona Creek vs Condamine Farms — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cona Creek, Queensland and Condamine Farms, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cona Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Condamine Farms (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cona Creek or Condamine Farms safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cona Creek has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Condamine Farms scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cona Creek scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Condamine Farms scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cona Creek or Condamine Farms?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cona Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
